worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Hard data: Opus 4.7 uses 1.46x tokens for text and up to 3x for images at the same per-token price — this is the stealth price increase that builders need to budget for.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Important correction to the above — the 3x image cost only applies at higher resolutions, so the real-world impact depends heavily on your image size.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Opus 4.7 confidently stuck to wrong answers in real tests and had to be abandoned for 4.6 — the quality regression risk is getting field-confirmed, not just theorized.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Someone reverse-engineered Claude Code's full 512K-line codebase into a visual map — useful for anyone building on top of it or trying to understand its orchestration limits.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Manifest is an open-source model router that cuts AI agent costs up to 70% by routing cheap vs. expensive models per request — directly relevant to anyone managing API spend.

worthClicking.author: @kloss_xyz
worthClicking.why: Concrete demo of Codex producing a full product suite (iOS app, web app, pitch deck, landing page) in under 2 hours — useful framing for distribution-first indie builders.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick's framing that releasing o1 was OpenAI's single most strategically costly decision — gave DeepSeek and competitors the reasoner blueprint — is a sharp lens on AI product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The 30-day roadmap rule is a pithy but defensible heuristic for AI-era indie product strategy, and the engagement (1145 likes, 67 reposts) suggests it's resonating broadly.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The argument that all existing paradigms (finance, comms, time management) must be reinvented from first principles for agents is a clean strategic frame for product positioning.

voices.why: Doing careful empirical work on Opus 4.7 token costs with actual tooling — the only voice in this feed producing verifiable, correctable signal rather than vibes.
Sharp, concise takes on agent-native product strategy and distribution that are actually original, not recycled tech-bro content.
Brings academic rigor to AI strategy questions; the o1 release framing this run is the kind of analysis useful for competitive positioning decisions.

examples: aakashgupta posting 30+ off-topic threads (NASA, Dragon Ball, tennis longevity, Wolf of Wall Street, Ticketmaster, bones healing)
RoundtableSpace crypto shilling (AAVE exploit, Polymarket trading bots, 'what are we aping in')
engagement-bait filler posts (GM CT, LOCK TF IN, WHAT'S COOKING IN THE TRENCHES)
